entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 15 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 15 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-15 07:36AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Lock in 43 per cent emissions cut and then build on it
  • ‘I can get superstars on the phone, but I couldn’t get my own Instagram back’
  • Queensland woman jailed for slashing man with broken bottle
  • Johns reveals 'insulting' trigger for post-Origin blow-up
  • Truck carrying chemicals crashes in Melbourne
  • Plans for new Brisbane Olympic basketball venue revealed
  • Queensland teen jailed for car jacking, dragging woman hundreds of metres
  • Eight people charged over Brisbane Airport freight theft ring
  • The Wrap: Sluggish China GDP weighs on the ASX
  • ‘He’s a natural’: Marshall backed to thrive as a Tigers coach
  • Threat of hazardous fumes from Tullamarine truck crash ‘eliminated’
  • Australia news LIVE: Leaked Labor climate change bill to enshrine 43 per cent emissions reduction target; Australian unemployment falls to 50-year low
  • Rocky start for Haotong Li at The Open
  • Man jailed for beating, stabbing murder of on and off girlfriend
  • Bracing for a long war, Macron warns people of power rationing
  • Albanese’s 2030 emissions target rejected by climate experts
  • Inside ‘Eddie Week’ and why it meant so much to the Randwick faithful
  • ‘Better rather than bitter’: Booktopia founder defends his legacy after sacking
  • Software company WiseTech shares jump on earnings upgrade
  • Sentence reduced for Oatlands driver who killed four children
  • Putin’s war aggravates Australia’s economic challenges: Treasurer
  • Best of cartoons, July 15, 2022
  • Watt rejects call to close Indonesian border over foot and mouth outbreak
  • Philip Lowe and Reserve Bank about to face a moment of truth
  • Albanese leaves door open to pandemic payment restart as South Australia joins push
  • Our attempt at living with COVID is a crisis, it’s time for clear messaging
  • Albanese declares ‘climate emergency’ as Pacific leaders tell Australia to do more
  • Why this Spanish energy tycoon is Australia’s fortune teller
  • Labor’s plan to cut carbon pollution faces major integrity test
  • ‘No time for politics’: Housing ministers to meet to tackle affordability and homelessness
  • Scott Morrison accuses China of supporting Putin’s war crimes

  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson

  • WAR CRIMES IN UKRAINE - The Torture Cell

  • Secret Service erased text messages from Capitol attack

  • Italian government on brink of collapse as PM Draghi offers resignation

  • ‘A half-carat isn’t going to cut it’: The Millennials sparking a diamond revolution

  • The cosmos reveals itself. Have we lost the ability to be astounded?
  • A symbol of summer hedonism - no wonder I was seduced

  • Large Melbourne school cancels classes, warns remote learning may be needed
  • First known sighting of Type C killer whales in Victoria
  • Corner office makes a comeback as city firm ditches hot desking

  • Crypto trading volumes tumble to two-year low
  • Agency targets Musk’s disclosures about Twitter
  • US banks prepare for downturn but don’t see recession yet
  • My colleagues and I wished the office was less boring. Careful what you wish for

  • ‘Wine helps’: Top crime writers share their secrets for a bestseller

  • ‘I felt such shame’: Manjula Datta O’Connor on the fight for daughters
  • American Gigolo official trailer
  • Kate Bush surprises Brisbane choir with email praising their cover of ‘Running Up That Hill’
  • How to Change Your Mind offical trailer
  • Ryan Gosling says Ken in Barbie has 'no money, no job, no car, no house'
  • Picture Perfect | LG's new 2022 OLED Review
  • How thunderstorms in Fiji are melting Antarctica’s glaciers
  • Mangroves in need of more research to protect a key part of Kimberley culture
  • Hiding in plain sight: Scientists discover an extra 76,000 hectares of mangroves in WA

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 15 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Vinnytsia strike work of ‘terrorist state,’ Zelensky says; Griner trial continues
  • The Vatican is trying to fight abuse. A case in Congo raises warning signs.
  • Sri Lanka president finally resigns after fleeing to a second country
  • Brittney Griner set for a fourth day of hearings in Russia drug trial
  • China’s economic growth slows to 0.4% after covid lockdowns
  • Eugenio Scalfari, elder statesman of Italian journalism, dies at 98
  • Secret van Gogh self-portrait discovered by X-ray of another painting
  • Biden’s first Mideast trip as president
  • Susie Steiner, author of stylish British crime thrillers, dies at 51

  • Tops market to reopen in Buffalo, two months after racist massacre
  • New York bodega worker’s murder charge sparks self-defense debate
  • Three people not yet located after flooding in rural southwest Va., police say
  • A CEO stood trial twice in Calif. slaying. He’s now charged again.
  • A small bookstore was scammed. The local community stepped in to save it.
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says U.S. is ‘not going to wait forever’ for Iran to rejoin nuclear deal
  • Trump called member of White House support staff amid Jan. 6 probe
  • About the Uber Files investigation
  • In Hollywood, a crypto platform that isn’t just a way to lose your savings
  • Stocks stumble amid interest rate, inflation worries
  • Calculate how much more mortgages will cost as interest rates rise
  • Mortgage rates spike as concerns about inflation persist

  • Fertilizer crisis delivers profits and pain as Ukraine fallout broadens

  • A Chevy Chase family refreshes a stately Colonial
  • Former medical clinic space converted to housing on Capitol Hill

  • A Tory Dark Horse’s Momentum Draws Fire From the Right
  • No, BMW, We Shouldn’t Have to Pay Extra to Heat Our Seats
  • Who Has the Stomach — Or Obstinacy — to Stand Against the Dollar?
  • Why India’s World-Beating Growth Isn’t Creating Jobs
  • How China’s Property Developers Got Into Such a Mess: QuickTake
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Crisis and What It Means: QuickTake
  • Forget pronouns. Democrats need to become the party of building things.
  • A Berkeley professor’s Senate testimony didn’t go how the left thinks it did
  • Putin’s long game in Ukraine may not play out as he predicts
  • Liberals can compromise on the election case at the Supreme Court
  • Is the economy good or bad? In a word: Yes.
  • Mr. President, I read the polls. Democrats don’t want you to run again.
  • Harry Truman bucked inflation. Both parties can learn from him.
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • James Webb Space Telescope images ranked by how good they look to eat
  • Why are Trump and Musk feuding? They’re the same.
  • There’s still plenty to learn about Jan. 6. More witnesses must talk.
  • Texas’s new lawsuit against Biden shows our dark post-Roe future
  • Here’s how to make red-flag laws most effective against gun violence

  • Sorry, protesters. Picketing a restaurant won’t stop gentrification.
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 15 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • The Open 2022: second round updates from St Andrews – live!
  • China slowdown adds to global economy fears, and European car sales plunge – business live

  • Samba Tri and ‘uncle’ Ronaldinho: why Raphinha was set on Barça move | David Hytner
  • ‘The NBA’s Coachella’: how Summer League became an unmissable event | Claire de Lune
  • Metal, cosmic barbarians and a judge called Dick Boner: the glorious grotesquery of Gwar
  • Officials warned of ‘serious wildlife incidents’ at Queen’s Sandringham estate
  • ‘Change is possible’: meet the Gen-Zers who embrace climate optimism
  • You be the judge: should I let my boyfriend have three games consoles?
  • Am I Normal? by Sarah Chaney review – it’s ok to be strange
  • ‘Horror and comedy were a saving grace’: facing her anger gave Elf Lyons the last laugh
  • Country Queen: first Netflix series to be produced in Kenya hits the screen
  • Pinnies, pancakes, pink: all hail the new action hero!
  • There are Tories of diverse origins and skin tones. What they need now is real difference | Nesrine Malik

  • Sri Lanka’s president Gotabaya Rajapaksa officially resigns
  • G20: Canada claims Russian delegation are personally responsible for ‘war crimes’ in Ukraine

  • Schlager louts? Row erupts over ‘sexist’ pop hit in Germany
  • ‘He’s maybe got a few screws loose’: the slab surfer taking extreme sport to a whole new level | Kieran Pender

  • Elephant and baby saved in dramatic rescue from manhole in Thailand
  • Russia-Ukraine war update: what we know on day 142 of the invasion
  • Zelenskiy condemns Russian ‘terrorism’ as Vinnytsia attack kills more than 20
  • The Uber files: firm knew it launched illegally in Australia, then leaned on governments to change the law
  • Secret Service agents’ January 6 texts were erased after oversight request
  • Full-length trailer for Amazon’s £1bn Lord of the Rings prequel is released
  • Alcohol is never good for people under 40, global study finds
  • ‘We have to try and win’: Shiels urges Northern Ireland to take on England
  • Man acquitted of bombing 1985 Air India plane shot dead in Canada
  • France sink Belgium to reach last eight but face anxious wait over Katoto injury
  • Pacific leaders welcome Australia’s ‘renewed commitment’ to climate change
  • ‘I’ve had enough’: Eddie Jones calls for global summit on future of rugby union
  • Ivana Trump, first wife of Donald Trump, dies aged 73
  • Constance Wu says she attempted suicide after Twitter backlash in 2019
  • Italian prime minister Mario Draghi to resign after coalition partner snub
  • Topley leads England to second ODI win over India on World Cup anniversary
  • Revealed: two members of Paris final ‘independent’ review worked for Uefa

  • Rival camps in UK leadership race aim fire at Penny Mordaunt
  • England’s Sarina Wiegman to avoid big changes for Northern Ireland game
  • Social media posts chart life and death of girl in Russian strike
  • Suella Braverman exits stage right, but the Mad Hatter’s Tory tea party goes on and on | Polly Toynbee
  • Bergamaschi’s strike denies Iceland and salvages precious point for Italy
  • The Guardian view on the Tory leadership contenders: not up to the job | Editorial
  • Joe Biden defends human rights record ahead of Saudi visit
  • My husband and I have separated. So why do I find it impossible to get a divorce? | Leading questions
  • Shanghai declares third rare extreme heat warning of summer
  • Child of refugees becomes Germany’s first black female cabinet minister
  • ‘Game over’: Steve Bannon audio reveals Trump planned to claim early victory
  • Way to go: why Germany’s €9 travel pass is a big step in the right direction | Melissa and Chris Bruntlett
  • Let’s rebuild the US microchip industry – not give it a $50bn-plus check | Bernie Sanders
  • ‘My voice lends itself to sadness​ –​ I carry a lot of grief’​: Rebekah Del Rio, David Lynch’s musical muse​
  • Mark Haddon pledges all future US royalties to abortion rights groups
  • Revealed: Queen’s sweeping immunity from more than 160 laws
  • Tom Courtenay: ‘I told Daniel Craig I turned down Bond because of the gym work’
  • The struggle to get a good holiday meal is real. Plan ahead | Jay Rayner

  • Dizziness, headache, dry skin … how to spot heatstroke – and avoid it in the first place

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 15 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Former rugby league star Tuivasa-Sheck poised for All Blacks debut
  • Yellen blasts Russian officials for continued support of Putin
  • Worker dies after being hit by timber fragment; 30th workplace fatality this year
  • Analysis: Macro gamblers grapple with immovable Bank of Japan

  • Richemont posts 12% Q1 sales increase as markets outside Asia grow

  • UK fast-charging battery startup Nyobolt raises $59 million

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 15 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 15 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-15 07:04AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong protests: top court acquits first person jailed for carrying zip ties, rules items ‘not instruments fit for unlawful purposes’
  • Hong Kong health officials say only some of over 250 people convicted of breaching Covid quarantine rules involved wristband data
  • Hong Kong winds up Target Insurance after industry regulator finds the taxi insurer to be insolvent, flexing its muscle for the first time
  • Coronavirus: online booking system for PCR tests halves morning wait time at Shenzhen checkpoint for Hong Kong cross-border travellers, but queue lengthens by afternoon
  • Three unconventional ways for Hong Kong to tackle its housing crisis in the near term
  • Hong Kong is stepping up Covid fight by issuing electronic wristbands, quarantining outbound travellers. Here’s what you need to know
  • Hong Kong needs young people who can imagine the future to keep the city competitive and thriving
  • Hong Kong schools to relocate to new development areas to combat shrinking student numbers, education minister says
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong researchers create algorithm to predict effectiveness of vaccines against variants with 95 per cent accuracy
  • Wharf Reic warns of interim loss as value of investment properties erodes amid poor business, economic conditions
  • Hong Kong’s Democratic Party cancels banquet amid Covid-19 concerns and criticism in pro-Beijing newspapers
  • Hong Kong housing shortage: government acts on John Lee pledge to ‘spare no effort’, sets stage for tenfold increase in homes to be built in second quarter
  • Young, poor and stuck: what can Hong Kong do to help those trapped in cycle of poverty?
  • Hong Kong protests: 6 Hong Kong fugitives in failed escape bid to Taiwan admit trying to hinder criminal proceedings
  • 1.16 tonnes of drugs seized at Hong Kong airport in first half of 2022, double that of same period in 2021
  • China home prices fall for 10th month as property crisis deepens amid economic slowdown, credit crunch and mortgage boycott
  • Foxconn risks fine from Taipei after taking minority stake in Chinese chip champion Tsinghua Unigroup
  • China’s President Xi visits Xinjiang for first time in eight years, praises region’s ‘core hub’ role
  • China GDP: second-quarter economic growth plunges to 0.4 per cent, lowest in 2 years after missing expectations
  • Macau casinos face delayed recovery amid citywide Covid-19 lockdown, high interest rate environment, rating companies say
  • China’s tech boomtown Shenzhen struggles under weight of lost jobs, dwindling profits and demand woes
  • China’s uncorrelated stocks are turning into a one-way bet for money managers while policy, regulatory risks stay elevated
  • China hosts its own Pacific island talks on last day of regional forum
  • Chinese ‘superiority’ keeps its inflation low, economic planner boasts as US prices keep rising
  • Study in China finds healthy thin people eat and exercise less rather than burning off excess calories
  • China facing more economic woes ahead of GDP release with mortgage boycott in over 80 cities
  • Weibo vows to regulate homonyms, ‘misspelt’ words if they are used to evade China’s strict censorship
  • China’s powerful sun-gazing telescope ready for launch
  • On Russian oil, China shuns US call to cap prices and urges dialogue in ‘very complicated’ issue
  • Covid-19: Beijing compound staff meet backlash after demanding quarantined residents wear wristband monitor
  • Shanghai game company hit with 1.1 million yuan fine for publishing unlicensed titles
  • China heatwave brings record high temperatures to Shanghai and other cities
  • Chinese province of Hebei detains hundreds in anti-crime blitz prompted by attack on women in restaurant
  • Hong Kong banks face higher risks on mainland lending as China’s economy slows, property woes deepen, Moody’s says
  • Chinese premier says discrimination against job applicants who have had Covid will be punished
  • Sri Lanka to get new president in 7 days as Rajapaksa’s resignation accepted
  • G20 talks in Bali overshadowed by Ukraine war as host Indonesia seeks progress on tackling global threats
  • Why new details about Indonesia’s criminal code revamp are worrying President Joko Widodo’s critics
  • Ripudaman Singh Malik, man acquitted in 1985 Air India bombing, shot dead in Canada
  • Britain and Japan set to merge military fighter programmes costing ‘tens of billions of dollars’, sources say
  • Japan PM Kishida blames police for death of former leader Shinzo Abe
  • UAE to invest US$2 billion in India for ‘food parks’ amid growing global food insecurity
  • Did Nepal’s China-India balancing act cause it to ditch the US-run SPP security initiative?
  • Coronavirus: Fresh Covid-19 wave sweeps Asia, NZ warns of pressure on hospitals
  • Smile or be fired, Philippine mayor tells employees
  • Sri Lanka crisis: President Gotabaya Rajapaksa emails resignation after fleeing to Singapore
  • Elon Musk’s dad, 76, has another child ... with his 35-year-old stepdaughter
  • Van Gogh self-portrait – with left ear intact – found hidden behind another painting
  • Crazy Rich Asians star Constance Wu says she attempted suicide after Twitter backlash
  • Ivana Trump, first wife of Donald Trump, dead at 73
  • Italy’s PM Mario Draghi offers to quit, but president rejects his resignation
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky urges ‘special tribunal’ into Russian invasion following latest deadly attack
  • ‘No nuclear Iran’ Israeli PM vows after Biden talk to strengthen alliance
  • Kevin Spacey pleads not guilty to sex offences, faces trial next year
  • How investing in sustainable infrastructure can help mitigate the effects of climate change and urban growth
  • Hong Kong stocks face worst week since March as China’s growth underwhelms while Alibaba tumbles amid regulatory woes
  • Ending Russia’s grain blockade won’t solve global food crisis
  • KKR to buy 25 per cent stake in Li Ka-shing-backed British water company for US$1.02 billion
  • TSMC posts robust quarterly earnings but sees chip inventory building up at some customers amid supply chain adjustment
  • What are VTubers? Japan’s virtual YouTubers are taking over the world, says Sony, and are ‘an inspiration to others’
  • Favourite Hong Kong restaurants for Mexican food, vegan and vegetarian bites, and ‘jaw-dropping’ doughnuts of a branding veteran and NFT developer
  • Netflix drama review: Mom, Don’t Do That! – Billie Wang, Alyssa Chia lead Taiwanese story about grief, loss and dating
  • Japanese Netflix romance reality show The Future Diary puts two polite men and one wholesome woman in a weird love triangle
  • Should you drink during a meal? Experts say it’s OK, but stick to one glass of lukewarm water
  • Alligators, thunderstorms, flash floods: Where the Crawdads Sing cast and crew talk about filming in New Orleans
  • Netflix movie review: The Gray Man – Chris Evans, Ryan Gosling in entertaining action thriller from Avengers: Endgame directors’
  • 5 mindful ways to practise self-care and relieve stress this summer if you’re stuck in Hong Kong
  • How to fight climate change: re-wear your clothes – if we all used them for twice as long as now, emissions from clothing would fall 44 per cent
  • How the words ‘monsoon’ and ‘trade wind’ blew into the English language
  • K-drama star Seo In-guk on new single album Love&Love and why he enjoys his Cafe Minamdang role so much
  • Meet Akie Abe, Shinzo Abe’s widow and beloved first lady of Japan: not like other PM’s wives, the former radio DJ opened her own izakaya, posted Instagram PDA and supported LGBT rights
  • Who was Ivana Trump, Donald Trump’s first wife? Ivanka, Donald Jr. and Eric lovingly remember the former skier, model and entrepreneur matriarch after her death at age 73
  • Meet Jung Eugene, star of Netflix’s Remarriage and Desires: she debuted as a model through YG KPlus like Nam Joo-hyuk, then cut her teeth in acting with the likes of Lee Jong-suk – and now Park Hoon
  • Which luxury fashion brand will Lanvin Group acquire next? Inside the upcoming plans of China’s Fosun International Ltd., from New York Stock Exchange additions to breaking into the Gen Z market
  • STYLE Edit: Richard Mille unveils dazzling new diamond-set versions of the RM 72-01 Lifestyle In-House Chronograph, shining a light on one of the watchmaker’s most unique timepieces
  • 5 best British royal wedding dresses of all time, from Queen Elizabeth’s pearls and Meghan Markle’s Givenchy veil, to Kate Middleton’s Alexander McQueen creation and Princess Diana’s iconic tiara
  • Who are Elvis Presley’s grandchildren, and where are they now? Lisa Marie, his only child, had 4 kids – actress Riley Keough, the late Benjamin Keough and twins Finley and Harper Lockwood
  • Meet Mathilde Pinault, heiress to the Kering fashion empire: the equestrian daughter of billionaire François-Henri Pinault admires Hollywood stepmum Salma Hayek, and is a Paris Fashion Week regular
  • Dumped, sued and exposed: is this Elon Musk’s worst week yet? From that Twitter lawsuit and his secret twins revelation with Shivon Zilis, to girlfriend Natasha Bassett breaking up with him
  • Wimbledon wives! Jelena Djokovic and Mery Perelló’s fashion, compared: Novak Djokovic and Rafael Nadal may be tennis greats, but their partners are style icons decked in Chanel, Cartier and Balenciaga
  • 5 designers giving traditional Asian fashions a modern update: Star Wars’ Kelly Marie Tran wore an ao dai by Thai Nguyen to the Oscars 2022, while Rihanna donned a Guo Pei look at the Met Gala
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 15 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Abe murder suspect planned bomb attack on religious leader

  • Dollar climbs further, pinning euro, yen at multi-decade lows
  • Cybozu to pay employees up to 150,000 yen to ease rising prices

  • China’s economy shrinks 2.6% during virus shutdowns

  • South Korea seeks to kickstart talks to resolve historical feuds with Japan
  • North Korea backs independence of breakaway regions in Ukraine
  • South Korea spy agency searched amid squabbling over North Korea cases
  • Sri Lankan president resigns, Parliament to convene Saturday
  • In Ukraine war, a race to acquire smarter, deadlier drones
  • U.S., allies aim to cap Russian oil prices to hinder invasion
  • Panasonic selects Kansas for vehicle battery mega-factory
  • Panel calls for antigen tests for COVID-19 to be available online
  • Ice cream made that stays frozen for slow-eating patients
  • TASTE OF LIFE: Udon noodles in cold soup flavored with egoma: Beat the summer heat with a dish that is simple to make, flavorful
  • EDITORIAL: Court ruling blaming TEPCO execs a warning to all nuke plants
  • EDITORIAL: Women make big gains in Upper House seats, but parity still far off
  • VOX POPULI: Late ‘thinker of the rice paddy’ no fan of farm modernization
  • VOX POPULI: Mori Ogai’s life demonstrates no one can see the end to all roads
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 15 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Biden’s Saudi Lesson: The Only Path Runs Through M.B.S.

entry.title: Taipei Times: 15 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
